slides.title: Your blood keeps a *three-month* sugar diary.
slides.sub: HbA1c reads the glucose stuck to your red blood cells. It can reveal what a single test misses.

slides.title: Sugar that sticks
slides.body: Glucose binds *permanently* to your red blood cells. The more sugar in your blood, the more of it gets coated over time.

slides.title: A built-in three-month log
slides.body: Red blood cells live about three months. So HbA1c reflects your *average glucose* over that whole window, not just one moment.

slides.title: Fasting tests can hide it
slides.body: A single fasting number can look fine while your average is creeping up. HbA1c is much *harder to fool*.

slides.title: Catching it early
slides.body: A rising HbA1c *is linked to* prediabetes years before symptoms. Caught early, the trend can often be turned around.

slides.title: Daily habits move the number
slides.body: Your A1c reflects months of small choices. Steadier meals, movement and sleep *can support* a healthier average.

slides.title: Steady days, *steadier* numbers.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the food, movement, steps and sleep behind your glucose, and rolls them into one daily Health Score you can actually act on.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
One fasting test can say you're fine while your three-month average quietly climbs. HbA1c tells the fuller story. 🩸🌿

Glucose sticks to your red blood cells for months, so your A1c is a tamper-proof average that can flag prediabetes early. The good news: daily habits move it. Save this. 🔖
